ucs.title: [LLM] Silk Typhoon (HAFNIUM) ProxyLogon — ASPX web shell dropped by Exchange IIS worker (w3wp.exe)
ucs.description: Hunts the canonical HAFNIUM post-exploitation artifact: the Exchange IIS worker process (w3wp.exe) writing an .aspx file under OWA/ECP/aspnet_client paths. This is the file-system fingerprint of CVE-2021-26855/26857/26858/27065 chained exploitation followed by China Chopper / ChinaChopper-style ASPX web shells (e.g. xlxhsdjxiwd.aspx, supp0rt.aspx, RedirSuiteServerProxy.aspx) that Microsoft and Mandiant attribute to Silk Typhoon / HAFNIUM (Operation Exchange Marauder).
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Filesystem.file_path) as file_path values(Filesystem.file_hash) as file_hash from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.process_name="w3wp.exe" Filesystem.file_name="*.aspx" (Filesystem.file_path="*\\FrontEnd\\HttpProxy\\owa\\auth\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\FrontEnd\\HttpProxy\\ecp\\auth\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\inetpub\\wwwroot\\aspnet_client\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Exchange Server\\V15\\ClientAccess\\Owa\\auth\\*") by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.user Filesystem.file_name Filesystem.process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime)
ucs.kql:
let HafniumPaths = dynamic([@"\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\owa\auth\", @"\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\ecp\auth\", @"\inetpub\wwwroot\aspnet_client\", @"\Exchange Server\V15\ClientAccess\Owa\auth\", @"\Exchange Server\V15\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\"]);
DeviceFileEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where ActionType in ("FileCreated", "FileModified", "FileRenamed")
| where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "w3wp.exe"
| where FileName endswith ".aspx" or FileName endswith ".asp" or FileName endswith ".ashx"
| where FolderPath has_any (HafniumPaths)
| where InitiatingProcessAccountName !endswith "$"
   or InitiatingProcessAccountName in~ ("system","network service","iusr")    // include service-context drops too
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, FileName, FolderPath, SHA256,
          InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          InitiatingProcessAccountName, InitiatingProcessAccountDomain
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let HafniumPaths = dynamic([@"\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\owa\auth\", @"\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\ecp\auth\", @"\inetpub\wwwroot\aspnet_client\", @"\Exchange Server\V15\ClientAccess\Owa\auth\", @"\Exchange Server\V15\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\"]);
ImFileEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventType in ("FileCreated","FileModified","FileRenamed")
| where ActingProcessName endswith "w3wp.exe"
| where TargetFileName endswith ".aspx" or TargetFileName endswith ".asp" or TargetFileName endswith ".ashx"
| where TargetFilePath has_any (HafniumPaths)
| project TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, ActorUsername, ActingProcessName,
          ActingProcessCommandLine, TargetFilePath, TargetFileName,
          TargetFileSHA256
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: HAFNIUM/Silk Typhoon ProxyLogon ASPX Web Shell Dropped by Exchange Worker
id: 6f3d7a2c-1c0e-4a4b-9f8b-7a1e2d3c4b5a
status: stable
description: Detects the IIS Exchange worker process (w3wp.exe) creating an ASPX file under OWA/ECP/aspnet_client directories — the canonical filesystem signature of HAFNIUM/Silk Typhoon ProxyLogon (CVE-2021-26855/26857/26858/27065) post-exploitation web-shell deployment.
references:
  - https://www.microsoft.com/security/blog/2021/03/02/hafnium-targeting-exchange-servers/
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0125/
author: SOC Detection Eng
date: 2026/05/08
tags:
  - attack.initial_access
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.t1190
  - attack.t1505.003
  - attack.g0125
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_proc:
    Image|endswith: '\w3wp.exe'
  selection_ext:
    TargetFilename|endswith:
      - '.aspx'
      - '.asp'
      - '.ashx'
  selection_path:
    TargetFilename|contains:
      - '\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\owa\auth\'
      - '\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\ecp\auth\'
      - '\inetpub\wwwroot\aspnet_client\'
      - '\ClientAccess\Owa\auth\'
  condition: all of selection_*
fields:
  - Image
  - TargetFilename
  - User
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate Exchange cumulative-update install events (rare, time-bounded — CU paths can be excluded by hash/signed-publisher)
level: high
ucs.rationale: This is HAFNIUM's defining artefact — Microsoft, Mandiant, Volexity and CISA AA21-062A all describe ASPX web shells (China Chopper variants, RedirSuiteServerProxy.aspx, supp0rt.aspx, etc.) being written into Exchange OWA/ECP auth folders by the IIS w3wp.exe worker as the immediate post-ProxyLogon outcome. It is highly specific to Silk Typhoon's Exchange-targeting tradecraft because: (1) the writin

ucs.title: [LLM] Silk Typhoon LSASS credential theft via rundll32 + comsvcs.dll MiniDump descending from w3wp.exe
ucs.description: Hunts the HAFNIUM/Silk Typhoon credential-access chain that follows ProxyLogon web-shell access: the Exchange IIS worker (w3wp.exe) spawns cmd.exe/powershell.exe which executes 'rundll32.exe C:\Windows\System32\comsvcs.dll MiniDump <lsass-pid> ...' (or Procdump -ma lsass) to dump LSASS to disk for offline credential extraction. Microsoft Threat Intelligence and Mandiant M-Trends specifically tie this living-off-the-land MiniDump pattern from an Exchange ancestry to G0125.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name IN ("rundll32.exe","RunDll32.exe") AND Processes.process="*comsvcs.dll*" AND (Processes.process="*MiniDump*" OR Processes.process="*#24*")) OR (Processes.process_name IN ("procdump.exe","procdump64.exe") AND Processes.process="*lsass*") OR (Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe") AND (Processes.process="*MiniDumpWriteDump*" OR Processes.process="*Out-Minidump*")) by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process Processes.process_name Processes.parent_process_name Processes.parent_process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | search (parent_process_name="w3wp.exe" OR parent_process="*w3wp.exe*" OR parent_process_name="cmd.exe" OR parent_process_name IN ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe")) | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime)
ucs.kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let ExchangeAncestors = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "w3wp.exe"
        or InitiatingProcessParentFileName =~ "w3wp.exe"
    | where FileName in~ ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","net.exe","whoami.exe","rundll32.exe")
    | project ShellTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, ShellPid = ProcessId,
              ShellImage = FileName, ShellCmd = ProcessCommandLine;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
     or AccountName in~ ("system","network service","iusr","iwam")
| where (
      (FileName =~ "rundll32.exe"
        and ProcessCommandLine has "comsvcs.dll"
        and (ProcessCommandLine has "MiniDump" or ProcessCommandLine has "#24"))
   or (FileName in~ ("procdump.exe","procdump64.exe")
        and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("lsass","-ma"))
   or (FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe")
        and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("MiniDumpWriteDump","Out-Minidump","dbghelp.dll"))
  )
| join kind=inner ExchangeAncestors on DeviceId
| where Timestamp between (ShellTime .. ShellTime + 30m)
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, AccountDomain,
          DumpProc = FileName, DumpCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
          ShellImage, ShellCmd, ShellTime,
          DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', Timestamp, ShellTime),
          ParentImage = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          GrandparentImage = InitiatingProcessParentFileName
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let ExchangeAncestors = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where ParentProcessName endswith "\\w3wp.exe"
    | where NewProcessName endswith "\\cmd.exe"
        or NewProcessName endswith "\\powershell.exe"
        or NewProcessName endswith "\\pwsh.exe"
        or NewProcessName endswith "\\net.exe"
        or NewProcessName endswith "\\whoami.exe"
        or NewProcessName endswith "\\rundll32.exe"
    | project ShellTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, ShellPid = NewProcessId,
              ShellImage = NewProcessName, ShellCmd = CommandLine;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
| where EventID == 4688
| where (
      (NewProcessName endswith "\\rundll32.exe"
        and CommandLine has "comsvcs.dll"
        and (CommandLine has "MiniDump" or CommandLine has "#24"))
   or (NewProcessName has_any ("\\procdump.exe","\\procdump64.exe")
        and CommandLine has_any ("lsass","-ma"))
   or (NewProcessName has_any ("\\powershell.exe","\\pwsh.exe")
        and CommandLine has_any ("MiniDumpWriteDump","Out-Minidump","dbghelp.dll"))
  )
| join kind=inner ExchangeAncestors on Computer
| where TimeGenerated between (ShellTime .. ShellTime + 30m)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, NewProcessName, CommandLine,
          ShellImage, ShellCmd, ShellTime,
          DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', TimeGenerated, ShellTime),
          ParentProcessName
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: HAFNIUM/Silk Typhoon LSASS Dump via comsvcs.dll MiniDump
id: 7a4e8b3d-2d1f-5b5c-af9c-8b2f3e4d5c6b
status: stable
description: Detects the rundll32.exe + comsvcs.dll MiniDump LOLBin pattern used by HAFNIUM/Silk Typhoon following ProxyLogon to extract LSASS memory for offline credential theft.
references:
  - https://www.microsoft.com/security/blog/2021/03/02/hafnium-targeting-exchange-servers/
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0125/
  - https://lolbas-project.github.io/lolbas/Libraries/comsvcs/
author: SOC Detection Eng
date: 2026/05/08
tags:
  - attack.credential_access
  - attack.t1003.001
  - attack.defense_evasion
  - attack.t1218.011
  - attack.g0125
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_rundll:
    Image|endswith: '\rundll32.exe'
    CommandLine|contains: 'comsvcs.dll'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'MiniDump'
      - '#24'
  condition: selection_rundll
fields:
  - Image
  - ParentImage
  - CommandLine
  - User
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate use of comsvcs.dll MiniDump by sysadmins or red-team tooling (rare)
level: high
ucs.rationale: Silk Typhoon's playbook documented by MSTIC, Mandiant, Volexity and CISA AA21-062A specifically calls out 'procdump.exe -ma lsass.exe' AND 'rundll32 C:\Windows\system32\comsvcs.dll MiniDump' chained from web-shell-spawned cmd.exe under w3wp.exe as the credential-access stage of ProxyLogon. The detection differentiates from generic comsvcs.dll abuse (which already has medium fidelity on its own) by
